summary.text: The dominant pattern today is a split focus between coding agent tooling (Cursor rules, SWE agents) and local LLM inference, with both categories showing sustained or breakout attention. ByteDance's UI-TARS-desktop continues its hot streak with 1,162 daily stars, reinforcing the AI browser/computer use category's staying power. Meanwhile, PatrickJS/awesome-cursorrules (7.1x acceleration) and gvergnaud/ts-pattern (15x acceleration) signal growing developer interest in customizing AI coding workflows. On Hacker News, the top-voted Ask HN thread (1,262 votes) asks about replacing Claude/GPT with local models, aligning with the breakout of ncnn (6x acceleration) and ts-pattern. The convergence of local inference projects and developer discourse suggests a shift toward self-hosted, offline-capable AI tools, though the data is still early.

projects.name: UI-TARS-desktop
projects.description: The Open-Source Multimodal AI Agent Stack: Connecting Cutting-Edge AI Models and Agent Infra

llmSummary.oneLiner: A multimodal AI agent stack from ByteDance that combines GUI automation, vision, and MCP tools into a CLI and desktop app for controlling computers and browsers.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It provides a unified open-source stack for building and running GUI agents that can see and interact with screens, browsers, and terminals, reducing the complexity of integrating vision-language models with real-world tool use.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 36,576 stars and a 24-hour delta of +1,162 stars, this project is experiencing sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike. The rule-based score of 66 and 'sustained_hot' trend type confirm consistent community interest over time.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent news includes a v0.3.0 CLI release (Nov 2025) with streaming and sandbox support, and a Beta announcement (Jun 2025). The README has been actively updated with new features like remote operators and UI-TARS-1.5 model support.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It bridges the gap between cutting-edge multimodal LLMs and practical desktop/browser automation, offering a free, open-source alternative to proprietary computer-use agents. The dual offering (CLI + desktop app) and MCP integration make it versatile for developers and power users.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a production-ready stack for building GUI agents with vision. Developers can use the CLI or SDK to automate complex workflows across browsers, desktops, and terminals, leveraging MCP for tool integration.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward open-source, multimodal agent frameworks that combine vision, GUI grounding, and tool-use protocols (MCP). ByteDance's investment suggests this is a strategic area for AI infrastructure, and the community adoption indicates strong demand for accessible computer-use agents.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers building automation agents, researchers in multimodal AI, power users wanting to automate desktop tasks, and teams integrating GUI agents into products.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on external LLM APIs (Volcengine, Anthropic) may introduce cost and latency. The project is still evolving rapidly (404 open issues) and may have breaking changes. Desktop app is early-stage (v0.2.0).

llmSummary.oneLiner: An open-source, animated, multi-language textbook for learning data structures and algorithms, designed for beginners.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a free, visually engaging, and interactive way to learn DSA with code that runs in multiple programming languages, reducing the barrier for beginners.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 127,336 stars and a 24-hour delta of +459, this project continues to attract massive daily attention. The acceleration is low (1), indicating steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike. It remains one of the most-starred educational repos on GitHub, consistently drawing new learners.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The last commit was on 2026-04-18, but there is no recent release or external buzz signal. The sustained star velocity suggests ongoing word-of-mouth and educational adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a universal need for accessible DSA education with high-quality animations and code examples in 12+ languages. The combination of a polished web book, downloadable PDF/EPUB, and active community contributions keeps it relevant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A prime example of how to build a successful open-source educational resource: focus on visual clarity, multi-language support, and community contributions. Developers can learn from its structure and engagement model.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, this repo signals that there is still high demand for foundational CS education content. It also shows that multi-language support and interactive elements (animations, runnable code) are key to broad adoption.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Students, self-taught programmers, and anyone preparing for technical interviews who wants a beginner-friendly DSA tutorial.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is not AI-specific; its category is 'Other AI' which may be a misclassification. The content is educational and unlikely to become obsolete quickly, but it competes with many other DSA resources.

projects.name: ts-pattern
projects.description: 🎨 The exhaustive Pattern Matching library for TypeScript, with smart type inference.

llmSummary.oneLiner: TS-Pattern brings exhaustive, type-safe pattern matching to TypeScript, enabling concise and readable branching on complex data structures with smart type inference.
llmSummary.problemSolved: TypeScript lacks native pattern matching, forcing developers to use verbose if/else or switch statements that are error-prone and lack exhaustiveness checking. TS-Pattern provides a compact, expressive API with full type safety and exhaustive matching.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo shows a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 3 (high), gaining 4 stars in the last 24 hours and maintaining a 7-day average of 1.5 stars/day. With 15,022 total stars and only 166 forks, the star-to-fork ratio is extremely high, indicating broad interest. The recent commit within 7 days and the sudden acceleration suggest renewed attention, possibly from a viral post or community highlight.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent commit and high acceleration may be due to a new feature or bug fix, but no release or external buzz is detected.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Pattern matching is a highly desired feature in TypeScript, and TS-Pattern is the most mature library offering it with exhaustive checking and tiny bundle size (~2kB). Its popularity is driven by the functional programming trend and the need for safer, more readable conditionals.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you work with complex state machines, reducers, or deeply nested data in TypeScript, TS-Pattern can drastically reduce boilerplate and eliminate runtime errors from unhandled cases.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a growing demand for functional programming patterns in TypeScript, especially for frontend state management. AI developers building type-safe agents or data pipelines can leverage TS-Pattern for robust branching logic.
llmSummary.targetUsers: TypeScript developers building frontend apps with complex state (React, Vue), functional programmers, and anyone tired of nested if/else or switch statements.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The library adds a runtime dependency (~2kB) and may have a learning curve for developers unfamiliar with pattern matching. Exhaustive checks only work with `.exhaustive()`, not `.otherwise()`, which could lead to incomplete coverage if misused.

projects.name: awesome-cursorrules
projects.description: 📄  Configuration files that enhance Cursor AI editor experience with custom rules and behaviors

llmSummary.oneLiner: A curated collection of .mdc configuration files that customize Cursor AI editor behavior for specific frameworks, libraries, and workflows.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using Cursor AI often get generic suggestions that don't respect project-specific conventions, architecture, or preferred libraries. This repo provides ready-to-use rule files that inject context (e.g., 'use getSession not getUser for auth') so the AI generates more relevant, consistent code with fewer manual corrections.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 39,842 stars and is accelerating sharply: 30 stars in the last 24 hours, 125 in the last 7 days, with a 7-day average of 16 stars/day and a 3-day average of 10.7 stars/day. The acceleration ratio of 2.8 (high) and the 'sudden_breakout' trend type indicate a recent spike in attention, likely driven by the growing adoption of Cursor AI and the need for project-specific rules.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-05-30) and includes new sponsorship banners and rule additions, but there is no release or external buzz event. The acceleration likely reflects broader Cursor AI adoption and word-of-mouth within developer communities.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As Cursor AI gains popularity, developers realize that generic AI suggestions fall short for complex projects. This repo fills a practical need by offering a community-maintained library of rules that make Cursor smarter about specific stacks (Next.js, React, Svelte, etc.), reducing friction and improving code quality.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Cursor AI, this repo is a shortcut to better AI suggestions. Drop the relevant .mdc files into your project's .cursor/rules/ directory to instantly align the AI with your tech stack and conventions.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'AI configuration as a shared asset' — teams are treating AI behavior as something that can be version-controlled, reviewed, and optimized just like code. Expect more curated rule collections and possibly a marketplace for AI editor rules.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using Cursor AI editor, especially those working with modern web frameworks (Next.js, React, Svelte, Angular, Astro, etc.) who want more accurate and context-aware AI code generation.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The rules are community-contributed and may become outdated as frameworks evolve. Some rules may conflict or be overly specific. No official Cursor endorsement. The repo's quality depends on ongoing maintenance.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with no third-party runtime dependencies and support for CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices like phones, embedded systems, and edge devices without heavy dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 7-day average star delta of 3.4 and an acceleration ratio of 6 (high), indicating a sharp increase in attention. With 23,380 stars and a recent release within 30 days, it's gaining momentum likely due to its practical utility for on-device AI deployment.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing improvements. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for easy PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which may have attracted new users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is trending because it offers a lightweight, dependency-free solution for running neural networks on mobile and edge devices, a critical need as AI moves to the edge. Its support for multiple backends (CPU, Vulkan) and frameworks (PyTorch, ONNX) makes it versatile.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building mobile or edge AI apps, ncnn provides a production-ready inference engine with minimal overhead and broad platform support. The pnnx tool simplifies model conversion from PyTorch/ONNX, lowering the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward on-device AI inference without cloud dependencies. Developers should consider ncnn for latency-sensitive or privacy-preserving applications on Android, iOS, and embedded Linux.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile app developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and developers working with Android, iOS, or Vulkan.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,183 open issues, indicating active development but also potential bugs or feature gaps. The C++ API may have a steeper learning curve for Python-only developers, though Python bindings exist.

projects.name: netdata
projects.description: The fastest path to AI-powered full stack observability, even for lean teams.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Netdata is an open-source, real-time infrastructure monitoring platform with per-second metrics, zero-configuration setup, and built-in ML-powered anomaly detection.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It solves the problem of complex, resource-heavy, and expensive monitoring tools by providing instant, high-resolution observability with minimal setup and low overhead, making full-stack monitoring accessible even for lean teams.
llmSummary.whyNow: Netdata is experiencing a sudden breakout with a 3.5x acceleration (high, >2.0) and a 7-day average star delta of 11.6, totaling 79,206 stars. The 24-hour delta of 37 stars indicates strong, sustained attention. This momentum suggests growing interest in AI-powered, real-time observability tools.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits within 7 days and a high acceleration ratio indicate active development and community engagement, but no specific release or external event is evident from the metadata.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Netdata is trending because it offers a unique combination of real-time per-second metrics, zero-configuration deployment, and edge-based ML anomaly detection, all in a single open-source package. Its energy efficiency and scalability appeal to DevOps and SRE teams seeking lightweight alternatives to Prometheus or Grafana.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Netdata provides a ready-to-use, high-resolution monitoring solution that can be deployed instantly on any infrastructure, reducing the need for custom instrumentation and complex pipelines. Its ML capabilities at the edge enable proactive issue detection without centralizing data.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Netdata signals a shift toward edge-based, real-time anomaly detection that doesn't require massive data lakes. It demonstrates that practical AI for observability can be lightweight, unsupervised, and integrated directly into existing monitoring stacks.
llmSummary.targetUsers: DevOps engineers, SREs, system administrators, and lean teams who need comprehensive, real-time infrastructure monitoring without the overhead of traditional tools like Prometheus or Grafana.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has no recent release, which may indicate a slower release cycle or focus on nightly builds. The high star count and community activity mitigate risk, but users should verify compatibility with their specific environments.

projects.name: Agent-Reach
projects.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents zero-config access to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — all free, no API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack native ability to read web content from platforms that require authentication, paid APIs, or complex scraping setups. Agent Reach solves this by providing a unified installer and channel router that selects the best free backend for each platform, handles authentication, and lets agents access content via simple commands.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 31,787 stars with a 24h delta of +2,809 and a 7-day delta of +7,284. The acceleration ratio is 3.3 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is a massive spike in attention, likely driven by viral sharing in AI developer communities.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-16). The project has a recent release and active commits within 7 days. The breakout may be fueled by word-of-mouth among Claude Code and Cursor users who need free web access for their agents.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: getting content from walled-garden platforms without paying for APIs or writing custom scrapers. The 'one CLI, zero API fees' pitch is compelling, and the project's design (multi-backend routing, self-diagnosis, easy install) reduces friction. The Chinese-language README also taps into a large developer base in China.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For AI agent builders, this is a ready-made capability layer that can be integrated into any agent that runs shell commands. It eliminates the need to build and maintain scrapers for multiple platforms. Developers can use it to give their agents web-reading abilities in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'capability layers' that abstract away the complexity of platform-specific access. Instead of each agent reinventing the wheel for web scraping, a shared open-source layer can handle authentication, backend selection, and fallback routing. This pattern may become standard for agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers and power users who use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or similar tools and need their agents to read content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and other platforms without paying for APIs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Reddit, Xiaohongshu), which carries account ban risk if detected. Some backends may break when platforms update their anti-scraping measures. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

projects.name: headroom
projects.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60–95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents and LLM applications waste tokens on verbose tool outputs, logs, and context, driving up cost and latency. Headroom provides a local-first compression layer (library, proxy, MCP server) that reduces token usage without sacrificing accuracy, and includes reversible compression (CCR) for retrieval when needed.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom has exploded from 0 to 30,563 stars, with a 24-hour delta of 1,743 stars and a 7-day delta of 15,900 stars. The acceleration ratio is 0.4 (high), indicating sustained, rapid growth. This is not a sudden spike but a consistent, massive influx of attention — likely driven by the practical need to reduce LLM costs in agentic workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The repository has recent releases and commits (pushed June 17, 2026), and the README mentions features like output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have broadened appeal. The sustained star velocity suggests word-of-mouth and community adoption rather than a single viral event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI developers: token costs. With 60–95% compression and reversible storage, it offers immediate ROI for anyone using LLMs in production. The multi-mode support (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) makes it easy to adopt without code changes, and the open-source model (Kompress-v2-base) adds credibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Headroom is a must-evaluate tool for any team using LLMs in production, especially with coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex. It can cut token costs by 60–95% with minimal integration effort, and the reversible compression ensures no data loss.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward token optimization as a core infrastructure layer for AI agents. Developers should expect compression to become a standard part of the LLM stack, similar to caching or load balancing. Headroom's approach — local-first, reversible, multi-algorithm — sets a template for future tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI engineers, ML ops teams, and developers using LLM-based coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot) or building RAG pipelines. Also relevant for anyone running LangChain, Agno, or custom LLM applications who wants to reduce token spend.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (30k stars in a short time) and may have undiscovered edge cases or bugs. The compression algorithms may not preserve accuracy for all content types (e.g., highly structured data). The output token reduction feature is experimental and uses estimates. Dependency on a custom Hugging Face model (Kompress-v2-base) could be a maintenance risk.

watchlistMovements.name: headroom
watchlistMovements.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60–95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents and LLM applications waste tokens on verbose tool outputs, logs, and context, driving up cost and latency. Headroom provides a local-first compression layer (library, proxy, MCP server) that reduces token usage without sacrificing accuracy, and includes reversible compression (CCR) for retrieval when needed.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom has exploded from 0 to 30,563 stars, with a 24-hour delta of 1,743 stars and a 7-day delta of 15,900 stars. The acceleration ratio is 0.4 (high), indicating sustained, rapid growth. This is not a sudden spike but a consistent, massive influx of attention — likely driven by the practical need to reduce LLM costs in agentic workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The repository has recent releases and commits (pushed June 17, 2026), and the README mentions features like output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have broadened appeal. The sustained star velocity suggests word-of-mouth and community adoption rather than a single viral event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI developers: token costs. With 60–95% compression and reversible storage, it offers immediate ROI for anyone using LLMs in production. The multi-mode support (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) makes it easy to adopt without code changes, and the open-source model (Kompress-v2-base) adds credibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Headroom is a must-evaluate tool for any team using LLMs in production, especially with coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex. It can cut token costs by 60–95% with minimal integration effort, and the reversible compression ensures no data loss.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward token optimization as a core infrastructure layer for AI agents. Developers should expect compression to become a standard part of the LLM stack, similar to caching or load balancing. Headroom's approach — local-first, reversible, multi-algorithm — sets a template for future tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI engineers, ML ops teams, and developers using LLM-based coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot) or building RAG pipelines. Also relevant for anyone running LangChain, Agno, or custom LLM applications who wants to reduce token spend.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (30k stars in a short time) and may have undiscovered edge cases or bugs. The compression algorithms may not preserve accuracy for all content types (e.g., highly structured data). The output token reduction feature is experimental and uses estimates. Dependency on a custom Hugging Face model (Kompress-v2-base) could be a maintenance risk.

modelDemoSignals.title: Variable-Width Transformers
modelDemoSignals.description: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. In this work, we empirically investigate nonuniform capacity allocation across network depth by proposing a $\times$-shaped > <former architecture. This design maintains wid
modelDemoSignals.summary: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. In this work, we empirically investigate nonuniform capacity allocation across network depth by proposing a $\times$-shaped > <former architecture. This design maintains wider early and late layers while narrowing the middle layers, utilizing a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism. Across decoder-only language models ranging from 200M to 2B parameters (dense) and 3B parameters (MoE), our > <former consistently outperforms parameter-matched uniform baselines on language modeling loss. By reducing the average layer width, this architecture also requires fewer overall FLOPs (22% reduction under fitted loss-matched scaling curves) and smaller KV cache memory and I/O cost (15% reduction). In analysis, we show that this bottleneck structure results in qualitatively different representations in residual streams. Overall, our results demonstrate that nonuniform width allocation can result in more resource-optimal scaling of language models.
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhaofeng Wu, Oliver Sieberling, Shawn Tan, Rameswar Panda, Yury Polyanskiy, Yoon Kim

modelDemoSignals.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
modelDemoSignals.description: Reproducing research results from papers and released code is central to scientific progress. Existing works have introduced benchmarks to evaluate whether LLM agents can assist with reproducibility, but they are difficult to scale due to their reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation and evaluation. We introduce ReproRepo, a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that leverages human-raised GitHub issues as naturally occurring supervision on realistic reproduction bloc
modelDemoSignals.summary: Reproducing research results from papers and released code is central to scientific progress. Existing works have introduced benchmarks to evaluate whether LLM agents can assist with reproducibility, but they are difficult to scale due to their reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation and evaluation. We introduce ReproRepo, a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that leverages human-raised GitHub issues as naturally occurring supervision on realistic reproduction blockers. We instantiate ReproRepo on 1,149 recent machine learning papers from major conferences and evaluate four frontier model-agent configurations. Our results show that LLM agents, even without executing code, can identify many real-world reproducibility problems from paper-repository pairs: the best agent in our study, namely Codex with GPT-5.5, surfaces at least one semantically related human-reported blocker for ~90% of papers in the study. Further analysis shows that agents are particularly effective for surfacing visible failures and identifying the right semantic region, but may still be insufficient in exact localization. ReproRepo can serve as a reusable, scalable framework for future evaluations of LLM agents on real-world reproducibility auditing. Our code is released at https://github.com/LithiumDA/ReproRepo.
modelDemoSignals.author: Shanda Li, Qiuhong Anna Wei, Jingwu Tang, Valerie Chen, Nihar B Shah, Tim Dettmers, Yiming Yang, Ameet Talwalkar
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.description: Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) requires embodied agents to explore and locate target objects without any prior training. modelDemoSignals.summary: Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) requires embodied agents to explore and locate target objects without any prior training. To this end, recent methods leverage foundation models. But they typically rely on static priors and lack adaptation, which leads to repeated errors and costly trial and error. In this paper, we propose a self-evolving ZS-OGN framework that enables continuous test-time improvement. Specifically, we build an agentic rule memory by extracting actionable knowledge from past trajectories. Then, we propose a retrieval strategy based on upper confidence bound, selecting effective rules by balancing semantic relevance and historical success. In addition, we introduce a memory-guided preflection module that forecasts potential outcomes before action, reducing inefficient exploration. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms existing zero-shot baselines, achieving a 10.1\% improvement in success rate with fewer unnecessary steps.
modelDemoSignals.author: Qi Chai, Wenhao Shen, Nanjie Yao, Yue Xia, Kaiyong Zhao, Jie Ma, Guosheng Lin, Hao Wang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, agentic

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
modelDemoSignals.description: With sophisticated cyber-attacks becoming increasingly prevalent, modern networks require intelligent autonomous cyber-defense agents trained via Reinforcement Learning (RL). These agents employ neurosymbolic approaches such as behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs) to learn, reason, adapt, and implement security rules while maintaining critical operations. However, these autonomous networks are partially observable systems, i.e., the cyber-attacker's (red agent's) actions are no
modelDemoSignals.summary: With sophisticated cyber-attacks becoming increasingly prevalent, modern networks require intelligent autonomous cyber-defense agents trained via Reinforcement Learning (RL). These agents employ neurosymbolic approaches such as behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs) to learn, reason, adapt, and implement security rules while maintaining critical operations. However, these autonomous networks are partially observable systems, i.e., the cyber-attacker's (red agent's) actions are not observable, making it difficult for the defender to predict red actions, learn red policies, or assess the attacker's intrusion levels. To address this, we propose a Policy Learning Technique using imitation learning to learn policies for partially observable RL agents with discrete states and discrete actions. We apply this technique in an autonomous cyber environment to predict red agent's actions from network observations and defender actions. Integrated with a neurosymbolic cyber-defense agent, our method effectively handles different red policies and achieves high prediction accuracy across diverse simulated scenarios.
modelDemoSignals.author: Ankita Samaddar, Sandeep Neema, Daniel Balasubramanian, Xenofon Koutsoukos

modelDemoSignals.title: Darshana Graph: A Parallel Commentary Corpus for Comparative Indian Philosophy, with Stylometric and Exploratory Graph Analyses
modelDemoSignals.description: We introduce Darshana Graph, a corpus of over 125,000 text records spanning classical Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, drawn from public-domain and openly licensed translations of sources including the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Sutras, principal Upanishads, the Pali Canon, and core Jain texts. Its distinctive contribution lies in a structurally unique subset of roughly 8,500 Hindu and Jain records in which the same root verse or sutra is aligned across eighteen historical commenta
modelDemoSignals.summary: We introduce Darshana Graph, a corpus of over 125,000 text records spanning classical Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, drawn from public-domain and openly licensed translations of sources including the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Sutras, principal Upanishads, the Pali Canon, and core Jain texts. Its distinctive contribution lies in a structurally unique subset of roughly 8,500 Hindu and Jain records in which the same root verse or sutra is aligned across eighteen historical commentators representing five schools of Vedanta and other darshanas, enabling direct comparison of how independent interpretive traditions read identical source material. To our knowledge, no publicly available resource provides comparable cross-commentator alignment at this scale. We present two analyses built on this corpus. First, a transparent stylometric comparison requiring no machine learning measures argumentative style through scriptural citation density, explicit refutation rate, and sentence complexity. It finds a moderate negative correlation between citation density and refutation rate, a marked increase in refutation rate across three commentators in a related doctrinal lineage, and measurable genre-level differences within the Pali Canon itself. Second, we describe a constrained large language model pipeline that extracts typed philosophical relationships between concepts using a predefined relation vocabulary and deterministic post-hoc validation. The resulting graph surfaces cross-school disagreement patterns while also revealing important extraction limitations, including cases where an independent embedding-based analysis disagrees with the graph-derived findings. We release the full corpus, extracted relationship graph, and all source code.
modelDemoSignals.author: Joy Bose
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, embedding, alignment

aihotHighlights.title: Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
aihotHighlights.summary: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。

aihotHighlights.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
aihotHighlights.summary: 火山引擎一站式营销创作平台Kickart 3.0（原"创作Agent"）正式上线，升级为对话式视频生成模式，用户可通过多轮对话调整商品图、故事板等，用自然语言生成营销视频。新增"爆款裂变"能力，上传视频链接后自动拆解爆款逻辑并重构至新商品视频，支持抖音电商内容合规与质量预审核。平台开放SaaS、API及Skill等多种交付方式，并已接入Seedance 2.0 mini，助力降低广告营销成本。

aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI 在 2026 年第一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超过同期 57 亿美元收入的一半。数据来自一份向股东披露的文件，直观体现 AI 大模型研发与规模化落地的巨额成本。OpenAI 正筹备上市，已在美国保密递交 IPO 申请，最早或于 9 月完成，估值最高可达 1 万亿美元。头部 AI 企业持续重金投入算力、模型研发与人才招募以维持竞争优势。

aihotHighlights.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
aihotHighlights.summary: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。

aihotHighlights.title: 中国加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织
aihotHighlights.summary: 中国正加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织，欢迎各方加入。2025年7月26日，中国政府倡议成立该组织，作为践行多边主义、推动共商共建共享全球治理的举措，旨在弥合数字和智能鸿沟、促进人工智能向善普惠发展。初步考虑总部设在上海。同日，2025世界人工智能大会发表《人工智能全球治理行动计划》，呼吁各方遵循向善为民、尊重主权、发展导向、安全可控、公平普惠、开放合作的原则，协力推进全球人工智能发展与治理。

aihotHighlights.title: baoyu-design 本地动画视频导出功能更新
aihotHighlights.summary: baoyu-design（本地运行 Claude Design 的 Skill）新增动画视频导出功能。其声明式动画引擎基于 f（t） 设计：任意时间点 t 可绝对确定画面状态。导出采用无头 Chromium 逐帧截图 + ffmpeg 编码，每帧等待两帧 requestAnimationFrame 确保渲染完成。截图以 2 倍 DPR（3840×2160）再缩回 1080p，保证细节清晰。95 秒 30fps 动画需 2850 次截图循环，帧帧精确。项目已开源（MIT），获 1.2K star。此前 baoyu-design 已支持 PPT 本地生成和导出可编辑 PPTX。

aihotHighlights.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
aihotHighlights.summary: 2026年Q1美国成人非虚构书籍销量同比下降9%，自助类下跌26.3%，仅手工艺/爱好/古董/游戏和宗教两个子类别增长。一位出版了多本《纽约时报》畅销书的作者透露，其五本书的印刷版年销量从2022年基准连续下滑：2023年-5%，2024年-13%，2025年-46%，2026年年化跌幅达-57% vs 2025年；若持续，2026年销量将比2022年减少约80%。所有格式在2025年下半年环比上半年下降约45%。作者认为，Claude、ChatGPT等大语言模型能提供更快、更便宜、更个性化的建议，正在系统性取代自助类书籍的"指令传递"价值。头部自助类品牌同期销量也下滑约40-60%，唯一显著变化是AI加速普及。作者判断整个指令传递型内容（包括YouTube教程、播客、在线课程、newsletter）都将面临替代。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅份额首超OpenAI，特朗普政府禁令反促采用量创新高
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅市场份额达41%，首次超越OpenAI（39.5%）。公司刚完成650亿美元融资、估值9650亿美元，并因首次盈利季度秘密提交IPO。特朗普政府以出口管制为由要求Anthropic禁止非美国人访问最新模型Mythos 5及Fable 5，导致两款模型下架。Ramp首席经济学家指出，类似争议（如3月被国防部列为供应链风险）反而推动Anthropic企业采用量创纪录。Ramp数据显示，企业支出主要流向Claude Opus模型（最新为Opus 4.8）。

aihotHighlights.title: Midjourney V8.1 推出 Draft mode 草稿模式与新功能预览
aihotHighlights.summary: Midjourney V8.1 的 Draft mode 草稿模式每次生成24张低分辨率低质量图片。用户可对任意图片点击 "Vary"，将其渲染为全质量、全分辨率版本。草稿任务消耗的快速小时数减半。

aihotHighlights.title: OpenAI 的领先优势正在快速缩小
aihotHighlights.summary: 评论认为 OpenAI 正面临多重危机：缺乏护城河导致市场领先地位下滑；最大投资者微软持续疏远，近期甚至公开考虑将主要产品外包给中国；亏损速度远超预期，年亏损额以 8 倍增长。华盛顿方面可能打压 Anthropic，但也可能反而帮助其崛起，而 Elon Musk 成为另一个潜在的竞标者。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic：智能体编码中专业知识回报持续存在
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic 基于约40万次 Claude Code 交互会话（2025年10月至2026年4月）分析发现：人类主导规划决策（做什么），Claude 主导执行决策（怎么做）。领域专业知识越强，模型每次指令完成的工作量越多。各类职业完成任务的成功率与软件工程师平均相近；领域专家成功率更高，但与中级用户差距不大。七个月间调试会话占比下降近一半，使用转向端到端智能体任务（部署运行代码、分析数据、编写非代码文档），典型任务价值平均上升约25%。

aihotHighlights.title: Meta 解散工程部门引发热议
aihotHighlights.summary: 6月16日，一篇标题为"Why is Meta destroying its engineering organization？"的博客文章出现在 Hacker News，获得110个点赞。文章指出 Meta 正在解散其工程组织，引发业界广泛讨论。具体原因和后续影响尚未明确。

aihotHighlights.title: 微软考虑为 Copilot Cowork 集成 DeepSeek V4
aihotHighlights.summary: 微软正考虑为 Copilot Cowork 提供微软托管的 DeepSeek V4 版本，作为更便宜的模型选项。Copilot Cowork 将放弃无限定价，转向按使用量计费，原因是成本过高（用户每周执行数百项任务导致费用激增）。若采用 DeepSeek，该模型将是可选的、经过微调与安全防护，并完全托管于 Azure。Axios 报道称微软已微调了一个可用模型，最终决定待定。

aihotHighlights.title: 公开聊天数据能否预测真实世界AI失调？
aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI利用WildChat公开数据集（2023年4月至2024年5月收集的100万条对话）模拟模型部署，预测GPT-5.1、GPT-5.2、GPT-5.4在真实生产环境中的不良行为率。与私有生产数据对比发现，WildChat模拟的平均预测误差约3倍；但对技术性和智能体型失调的预测精度下降。研究验证了公开数据集作为外部审计工具的可行性。
aihotHighlights.description: OpenAI利用WildChat公开数据集（2023年4月至2024年5月收集的100万条对话）模拟模型部署，预测GPT-5.1、GPT-5.2、GPT-5.4在真实生产环境中的不良行为率。与私有生产数据对比发现，WildChat模拟的平均预测误差约3倍；但对技术性和智能体型失调的预测精度下降。研究验证了公开数据集作为外部审计工具的可行性。

raw.titleEn: Can public chat data predict real-world AI misalignments？

items.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
items.summary: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。

crossSourceHighlights.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
normalizedKeys: title:人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了
title:has ai already killed self help nonfiction books

items.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
items.summary: 2026年Q1美国成人非虚构书籍销量同比下降9%，自助类下跌26.3%，仅手工艺/爱好/古董/游戏和宗教两个子类别增长。一位出版了多本《纽约时报》畅销书的作者透露，其五本书的印刷版年销量从2022年基准连续下滑：2023年-5%，2024年-13%，2025年-46%，2026年年化跌幅达-57% vs 2025年；若持续，2026年销量将比2022年减少约80%。所有格式在2025年下半年环比上半年下降约45%。作者认为，Claude、ChatGPT等大语言模型能提供更快、更便宜、更个性化的建议，正在系统性取代自助类书籍的"指令传递"价值。头部自助类品牌同期销量也下滑约40-60%，唯一显著变化是AI加速普及。作者判断整个指令传递型内容（包括YouTube教程、播客、在线课程、newsletter）都将面临替代。

items.title: Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
items.description: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。

items.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
items.description: 火山引擎一站式营销创作平台Kickart 3.0（原"创作Agent"）正式上线，升级为对话式视频生成模式，用户可通过多轮对话调整商品图、故事板等，用自然语言生成营销视频。新增"爆款裂变"能力，上传视频链接后自动拆解爆款逻辑并重构至新商品视频，支持抖音电商内容合规与质量预审核。平台开放SaaS、API及Skill等多种交付方式，并已接入Seedance 2.0 mini，助力降低广告营销成本。

items.title: 消息称 OpenAI 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超同期收入的一半
items.description: OpenAI 在 2026 年第一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超过同期 57 亿美元收入的一半。数据来自一份向股东披露的文件，直观体现 AI 大模型研发与规模化落地的巨额成本。OpenAI 正筹备上市，已在美国保密递交 IPO 申请，最早或于 9 月完成，估值最高可达 1 万亿美元。头部 AI 企业持续重金投入算力、模型研发与人才招募以维持竞争优势。

items.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
items.description: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。

items.title: 中国加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织
items.description: 中国正加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织，欢迎各方加入。2025年7月26日，中国政府倡议成立该组织，作为践行多边主义、推动共商共建共享全球治理的举措，旨在弥合数字和智能鸿沟、促进人工智能向善普惠发展。初步考虑总部设在上海。同日，2025世界人工智能大会发表《人工智能全球治理行动计划》，呼吁各方遵循向善为民、尊重主权、发展导向、安全可控、公平普惠、开放合作的原则，协力推进全球人工智能发展与治理。

items.title: baoyu-design 本地动画视频导出功能更新
items.description: baoyu-design（本地运行 Claude Design 的 Skill）新增动画视频导出功能。其声明式动画引擎基于 f（t） 设计：任意时间点 t 可绝对确定画面状态。导出采用无头 Chromium 逐帧截图 + ffmpeg 编码，每帧等待两帧 requestAnimationFrame 确保渲染完成。截图以 2 倍 DPR（3840×2160）再缩回 1080p，保证细节清晰。95 秒 30fps 动画需 2850 次截图循环，帧帧精确。项目已开源（MIT），获 1.2K star。此前 baoyu-design 已支持 PPT 本地生成和导出可编辑 PPTX。

items.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
items.description: 2026年Q1美国成人非虚构书籍销量同比下降9%，自助类下跌26.3%，仅手工艺/爱好/古董/游戏和宗教两个子类别增长。一位出版了多本《纽约时报》畅销书的作者透露，其五本书的印刷版年销量从2022年基准连续下滑：2023年-5%，2024年-13%，2025年-46%，2026年年化跌幅达-57% vs 2025年；若持续，2026年销量将比2022年减少约80%。所有格式在2025年下半年环比上半年下降约45%。作者认为，Claude、ChatGPT等大语言模型能提供更快、更便宜、更个性化的建议，正在系统性取代自助类书籍的"指令传递"价值。头部自助类品牌同期销量也下滑约40-60%，唯一显著变化是AI加速普及。作者判断整个指令传递型内容（包括YouTube教程、播客、在线课程、newsletter）都将面临替代。

items.title: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅份额首超OpenAI，特朗普政府禁令反促采用量创新高
items.description: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅市场份额达41%，首次超越OpenAI（39.5%）。公司刚完成650亿美元融资、估值9650亿美元，并因首次盈利季度秘密提交IPO。特朗普政府以出口管制为由要求Anthropic禁止非美国人访问最新模型Mythos 5及Fable 5，导致两款模型下架。Ramp首席经济学家指出，类似争议（如3月被国防部列为供应链风险）反而推动Anthropic企业采用量创纪录。Ramp数据显示，企业支出主要流向Claude Opus模型（最新为Opus 4.8）。

Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
items.description: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。

items.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
items.description: 火山引擎一站式营销创作平台Kickart 3.0（原"创作Agent"）正式上线，升级为对话式视频生成模式，用户可通过多轮对话调整商品图、故事板等，用自然语言生成营销视频。新增"爆款裂变"能力，上传视频链接后自动拆解爆款逻辑并重构至新商品视频，支持抖音电商内容合规与质量预审核。平台开放SaaS、API及Skill等多种交付方式，并已接入Seedance 2.0 mini，助力降低广告营销成本。

items.title: 消息称 OpenAI 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超同期收入的一半
items.description: OpenAI 在 2026 年第一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超过同期 57 亿美元收入的一半。数据来自一份向股东披露的文件，直观体现 AI 大模型研发与规模化落地的巨额成本。OpenAI 正筹备上市，已在美国保密递交 IPO 申请，最早或于 9 月完成，估值最高可达 1 万亿美元。头部 AI 企业持续重金投入算力、模型研发与人才招募以维持竞争优势。

trendEntities.summary: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Agent-Reach is an open-source CLI tool that provides AI agents with unified access to multiple social media and content platforms (Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu) via MCP (Model Context Protocol) without requiring API keys.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained 8,071 stars in the past week and 2,487 in the last 24 hours, indicating strong developer interest in MCP-based tool calling for AI agents. This aligns with the broader trend of enabling AI agents to interact with external services through standardized protocols.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI agent developers, MCP tool builders, developers building social media scraping or monitoring tools, and product managers exploring agentic workflows.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enables AI agents to access real-time social data without per-API costs, potentially reducing operational expenses for agent-based products. However, reliance on scraping may violate platform terms of service.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a ready-to-use MCP server for multiple platforms, simplifying integration of social data into AI agents. Developers can extend or customize the tool for their own use cases.
llmSummary.riskNotes: High star growth may be driven by novelty and marketing; actual production adoption is unverified. Scraping-based approach may face legal or rate-limiting issues. No evidence of commercial use or long-term maintenance commitment.

trendEntities.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before sending them to an LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95% while preserving answer quality. It offers a library, proxy, and MCP server.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant attention on GitHub with 30k+ stars and a 7-day delta of 15,900 stars, indicating strong developer interest in cost-saving LLM infrastructure. The concept of token compression addresses a growing pain point as LLM usage scales.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered applications, especially those using RAG or agentic workflows; engineering teams managing LLM API costs; and platform engineers optimizing AI infrastructure.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Reducing token consumption directly lowers API costs for LLM-dependent products, which is critical for scaling AI features economically.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in solution to cut token usage without changing prompts or models, potentially saving significant costs in production.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Compression may lose nuance in edge cases; production reliability and compatibility with various LLMs are unproven at scale. Stars reflect attention, not adoption.

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and reduces token usage by 60-90% for common developer commands, implemented as a single Rust binary with no dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (2.5k in 7 days) and high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers using LLM APIs frequently, engineering managers overseeing AI tooling budgets, and platform teams building internal developer tools.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to control LLM API costs in development workflows, potentially reducing expenses for teams with high API usage.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Offers a lightweight, drop-in solution to cut token consumption without changing existing tools or workflows, appealing to developers seeking immediate cost savings.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Token reduction claims (60-90%) may vary by use case; no independent benchmarks yet. Rapid star growth could be hype-driven. Single Rust binary may limit extensibility.

trendEntities.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
normalizedKeys: title:wolfram 语言和 mathematica 15 版发布 内置 ai 助手 符号音乐等新功能

items.title: Wolfram 语言和 Mathematica 15 版发布：内置 AI 助手、符号音乐等新功能
items.summary: 在 Mathematica 诞生近 38 年后，Wolfram 语言与 Mathematica 发布 Version 15。每个笔记本内置 AI 助手，支持从 AI 环境中直接调用 Wolfram 技术。新增符号音乐系统、大规模时间序列与事件序列处理、分类数据计算、模型拟合超函数 ModelFit。笔记本支持千兆字节级大小与实时查找，首次引入侧边栏、视觉主题及弃用功能样式。强化了表格连接、多点可视化、图形刻度绘制与轨道运行计算等功能。DSolve 拐角处获得 AI 方法辅助，支持偏微分方程曲线坐标求解。扩充了矩阵分解、多元 zeta 函数与调和数、流线型部分分式分解。强化了 WebSocket 实时连接、Python 交互改进，支持 CUDA 内核作为外部函数，Wolfram Compute Services 新增 GPU 支持。

trendEntities.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
normalizedKeys: title:人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了
title:has ai already killed self help nonfiction books

items.title: 人工智能是否已经让自助类非虚构书籍销声匿迹了？
items.summary: 2026年Q1美国成人非虚构书籍销量同比下降9%，自助类下跌26.3%，仅手工艺/爱好/古董/游戏和宗教两个子类别增长。一位出版了多本《纽约时报》畅销书的作者透露，其五本书的印刷版年销量从2022年基准连续下滑：2023年-5%，2024年-13%，2025年-46%，2026年年化跌幅达-57% vs 2025年；若持续，2026年销量将比2022年减少约80%。所有格式在2025年下半年环比上半年下降约45%。作者认为，Claude、ChatGPT等大语言模型能提供更快、更便宜、更个性化的建议，正在系统性取代自助类书籍的"指令传递"价值。头部自助类品牌同期销量也下滑约40-60%，唯一显著变化是AI加速普及。作者判断整个指令传递型内容（包括YouTube教程、播客、在线课程、newsletter）都将面临替代。

trendEntities.summary: 2026年Q1美国成人非虚构书籍销量同比下降9%，自助类下跌26.3%，仅手工艺/爱好/古董/游戏和宗教两个子类别增长。一位出版了多本《纽约时报》畅销书的作者透露，其五本书的印刷版年销量从2022年基准连续下滑：2023年-5%，2024年-13%，2025年-46%，2026年年化跌幅达-57% vs 2025年；若持续，2026年销量将比2022年减少约80%。所有格式在2025年下半年环比上半年下降约45%。作者认为，Claude、ChatGPT等大语言模型能提供更快、更便宜、更个性化的建议，正在系统性取代自助类书籍的"指令传递"价值。头部自助类品牌同期销量也下滑约40-60%，唯一显著变化是AI加速普及。作者判断整个指令传递型内容（包括YouTube教程、播客、在线课程、newsletter）都将面临替代。

trendEntities.title: Against essential and accidental complexity (2020)
Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
normalizedKeys: title:strands robots sdk 用单一智能体打通 hugging face hub 到物理机器人

items.title: Strands Robots SDK：用单一智能体打通 Hugging Face Hub 到物理机器人
items.summary: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。
items.description: AWS（Apache 2.0）开源的 Strands Robots SDK 将 LeRobot 栈封装为 AgentTools，构建统一智能体。默认用 MuJoCo 模拟（无需硬件），mode="real" 切换至真实机器人。可记录演示数据为 LeRobotDataset 并推送 Hugging Face Hub，运行 GR00T 或 LerobotLocal 策略推理，经 Zenoh mesh 广播命令到多台机器人。模拟与硬件代码完全一致，只需改一个关键字参数。示例可在笔记本（Python 3.12+，Linux/macOS）无硬件、无 GPU 运行。

trendEntities.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
normalizedKeys: title:kickart 3 0发布 让广告视频创作更精准高效

items.title: Kickart 3.0发布，让广告视频创作更精准高效
items.summary: 火山引擎一站式营销创作平台Kickart 3.0（原"创作Agent"）正式上线，升级为对话式视频生成模式，用户可通过多轮对话调整商品图、故事板等，用自然语言生成营销视频。新增"爆款裂变"能力，上传视频链接后自动拆解爆款逻辑并重构至新商品视频，支持抖音电商内容合规与质量预审核。平台开放SaaS、API及Skill等多种交付方式，并已接入Seedance 2.0 mini，助力降低广告营销成本。

trendEntities.title: 消息称 OpenAI 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超同期收入的一半
normalizedKeys: title:消息称 openai 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元 超同期收入的一半

items.title: 消息称 OpenAI 今年一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超同期收入的一半
items.summary: OpenAI 在 2026 年第一季度现金消耗达 37 亿美元，超过同期 57 亿美元收入的一半。数据来自一份向股东披露的文件，直观体现 AI 大模型研发与规模化落地的巨额成本。OpenAI 正筹备上市，已在美国保密递交 IPO 申请，最早或于 9 月完成，估值最高可达 1 万亿美元。头部 AI 企业持续重金投入算力、模型研发与人才招募以维持竞争优势。

trendEntities.title: 中国加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织
normalizedKeys: title:中国加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织

items.title: 中国加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织
items.summary: 中国正加紧筹建世界人工智能合作组织，欢迎各方加入。2025年7月26日，中国政府倡议成立该组织，作为践行多边主义、推动共商共建共享全球治理的举措，旨在弥合数字和智能鸿沟、促进人工智能向善普惠发展。初步考虑总部设在上海。同日，2025世界人工智能大会发表《人工智能全球治理行动计划》，呼吁各方遵循向善为民、尊重主权、发展导向、安全可控、公平普惠、开放合作的原则，协力推进全球人工智能发展与治理。

trendEntities.title: baoyu-design 本地动画视频导出功能更新
normalizedKeys: title:baoyu design 本地动画视频导出功能更新

items.title: baoyu-design 本地动画视频导出功能更新
items.summary: baoyu-design（本地运行 Claude Design 的 Skill）新增动画视频导出功能。其声明式动画引擎基于 f（t） 设计：任意时间点 t 可绝对确定画面状态。导出采用无头 Chromium 逐帧截图 + ffmpeg 编码，每帧等待两帧 requestAnimationFrame 确保渲染完成。截图以 2 倍 DPR（3840×2160）再缩回 1080p，保证细节清晰。95 秒 30fps 动画需 2850 次截图循环，帧帧精确。项目已开源（MIT），获 1.2K star。此前 baoyu-design 已支持 PPT 本地生成和导出可编辑 PPTX。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅份额首超OpenAI，特朗普政府禁令反促采用量创新高
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 5月企业ai订阅份额首超openai 特朗普政府禁令反促采用量创新高

items.title: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅份额首超OpenAI，特朗普政府禁令反促采用量创新高
items.summary: Anthropic 5月企业AI订阅市场份额达41%，首次超越OpenAI（39.5%）。公司刚完成650亿美元融资、估值9650亿美元，并因首次盈利季度秘密提交IPO。特朗普政府以出口管制为由要求Anthropic禁止非美国人访问最新模型Mythos 5及Fable 5，导致两款模型下架。Ramp首席经济学家指出，类似争议（如3月被国防部列为供应链风险）反而推动Anthropic企业采用量创纪录。Ramp数据显示，企业支出主要流向Claude Opus模型（最新为Opus 4.8）。

items.title: Midjourney V8.1 推出 Draft mode 草稿模式与新功能预览
items.summary: Midjourney V8.1 的 Draft mode 草稿模式每次生成24张低分辨率低质量图片。用户可对任意图片点击 "Vary"，将其渲染为全质量、全分辨率版本。草稿任务消耗的快速小时数减半。

trendEntities.title: OpenAI 的领先优势正在快速缩小
normalizedKeys: title:openai 的领先优势正在快速缩小

items.title: OpenAI 的领先优势正在快速缩小
items.summary: 评论认为 OpenAI 正面临多重危机：缺乏护城河导致市场领先地位下滑；最大投资者微软持续疏远，近期甚至公开考虑将主要产品外包给中国；亏损速度远超预期，年亏损额以 8 倍增长。华盛顿方面可能打压 Anthropic，但也可能反而帮助其崛起，而 Elon Musk 成为另一个潜在的竞标者。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic：智能体编码中专业知识回报持续存在
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 智能体编码中专业知识回报持续存在

items.title: Anthropic：智能体编码中专业知识回报持续存在
items.summary: Anthropic 基于约40万次 Claude Code 交互会话（2025年10月至2026年4月）分析发现：人类主导规划决策（做什么），Claude 主导执行决策（怎么做）。领域专业知识越强，模型每次指令完成的工作量越多。各类职业完成任务的成功率与软件工程师平均相近；领域专家成功率更高，但与中级用户差距不大。七个月间调试会话占比下降近一半，使用转向端到端智能体任务（部署运行代码、分析数据、编写非代码文档），典型任务价值平均上升约25%。

trendEntities.title: 微软考虑为 Copilot Cowork 集成 DeepSeek V4
normalizedKeys: title:微软考虑为 copilot cowork 集成 deepseek v4

items.title: 微软考虑为 Copilot Cowork 集成 DeepSeek V4
items.summary: 微软正考虑为 Copilot Cowork 提供微软托管的 DeepSeek V4 版本，作为更便宜的模型选项。Copilot Cowork 将放弃无限定价，转向按使用量计费，原因是成本过高（用户每周执行数百项任务导致费用激增）。若采用 DeepSeek，该模型将是可选的、经过微调与安全防护，并完全托管于 Azure。Axios 报道称微软已微调了一个可用模型，最终决定待定。

trendEntities.title: 公开聊天数据能否预测真实世界AI失调？
normalizedKeys: title:公开聊天数据能否预测真实世界ai失调

items.title: 公开聊天数据能否预测真实世界AI失调？
items.summary: OpenAI利用WildChat公开数据集（2023年4月至2024年5月收集的100万条对话）模拟模型部署，预测GPT-5.1、GPT-5.2、GPT-5.4在真实生产环境中的不良行为率。与私有生产数据对比发现，WildChat模拟的平均预测误差约3倍；但对技术性和智能体型失调的预测精度下降。研究验证了公开数据集作为外部审计工具的可行性。

trendEntities.title: GLM-5.2 上线并开源：专注 Coding 与长程任务
normalizedKeys: title:glm 5 2 上线并开源 专注 coding 与长程任务

items.title: GLM-5.2 上线并开源：专注 Coding 与长程任务
items.summary: GLM-5.2 已发布并开源，采用 MIT 协议，支持 1M 上下文窗口。Coding 方面能承载项目级上下文，长程任务执行更稳定，遵循生产级工程规范，并支持客户端与移动端真机调试闭环。通过极致 Infra 优化，发布首日即可在国产算力平台运行。模型已开源至 GitHub、Hugging Face、ModelScope、BigModel 开放平台、Z.ai、智谱清言、AutoClaw 及 ZCode。

trendEntities.title: 小米 MiMo Claw 正式版发布：旗舰模型+金山办公，全新订阅服务上线
normalizedKeys: title:小米 mimo claw 正式版发布 旗舰模型 金山办公 全新订阅服务上线

items.title: 小米 MiMo Claw 正式版发布：旗舰模型+金山办公，全新订阅服务上线
items.summary: 小米推出云端轻量化 Claw 类产品 MiMo Claw 正式版，搭载与 OpenClaw 框架深度适配的 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 旗舰模型。该模型原生兼容 MCP 工具调用协议，内置百万级超长上下文，支持单会话千次以上连续工具调用；依托 MTP 三层解码架构，在 OpenClaw 标准 Agent 工作流中吞吐效率提升约 3 倍。ClawEval 测试中任务达标率（Pass3）达 63.8%，Token 消耗较同类产品降低 40%-60%。联动金山办公生态，提供 Word、Excel、PPT、PDF 等格式的 AI 生成、预览与在线编辑一站式服务。免费用户每日单次体验时长从1小时升级至4小时，面向高频用户推出 TokenPlan 分层订阅（Lite/Standard/Pro/Max），支持灵活叠加，限时定价14.9元/月、19.9元/月、233.8元/年。

sourceItems.summary: 小米推出云端轻量化 Claw 类产品 MiMo Claw 正式版，搭载与 OpenClaw 框架深度适配的 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 旗舰模型。该模型原生兼容 MCP 工具调用协议，内置百万级超长上下文，支持单会话千次以上连续工具调用；依托 MTP 三层解码架构，在 OpenClaw 标准 Agent 工作流中吞吐效率提升约 3 倍。ClawEval 测试中任务达标率（Pass3）达 63.8%，Token 消耗较同类产品降低 40%-60%。联动金山办公生态，提供 Word、Excel、PPT、PDF 等格式的 AI 生成、预览与在线编辑一站式服务。免费用户每日单次体验时长从1小时升级至4小时，面向高频用户推出 TokenPlan 分层订阅（Lite/Standard/Pro/Max），支持灵活叠加，限时定价14.9元/月、19.9元/月、233.8元/年。

trendEntities.title: 美国司法部援引国家安全为xAI未经许可的燃气轮机辩护
normalizedKeys: title:美国司法部援引国家安全为xai未经许可的燃气轮机辩护

items.title: 美国司法部援引国家安全为xAI未经许可的燃气轮机辩护
items.summary: 美国司法部在一份驳回诉讼的动议中称，xAI的聊天机器人Grok对军事行动至关重要，以此为其在密西西比州Southaven的Colossus 2设施运行未经许可的燃气轮机辩护。NAACP已提起诉讼，指控xAI的燃气轮机数量从4月的27台增至57台，导致氮氧化物排放飙升111%。国防部首席数字与人工智能官Cameron Stanley表示，Grok是支持机密和绝密网络军事任务的四款AI模型之一，包括近期针对伊朗的打击。

trendEntities.title: WorkBuddy日活飙升至行业第二的3-4倍，非技术用户涌入
normalizedKeys: title:workbuddy日活飙升至行业第二的3 4倍 非技术用户涌入

items.title: WorkBuddy日活飙升至行业第二的3-4倍，非技术用户涌入
items.summary: 从3月至今，WorkBuddy日活用户数已达行业第二名的3-4倍，用户不再限于开发者，大量HR、运营、行政等非技术岗位也在使用。其企业版和项目功能进一步扩展了Agent办公场景。同期，Trae Work、QoderWork、Kimi Work等产品纷纷改名或出新，争夺市场。腾讯云认为这可能是十年一遇的机遇。

trendEntities.title: SpaceX 以 600 亿美元股票收购 AI 编程公司 Cursor
normalizedKeys: title:spacex 以 600 亿美元股票收购 ai 编程公司 cursor

items.title: SpaceX 以 600 亿美元股票收购 AI 编程公司 Cursor
items.summary: SpaceX 在历史性 IPO 数天后，同意以 600 亿美元股票收购 AI 编程初创公司 Cursor，旨在帮助其围绕 xAI 构建的 AI 部门追赶主要 AI 实验室。此前 Cursor 正接近完成一轮 20 亿美元融资，估值 500 亿美元，投资方包括 Andreessen Horowitz、Thrive 和 Nvidia。SpaceX 在 IPO 期间向投资者表示，其 AI 产品可寻址市场达 26 万亿美元。交易预计于今年第三季度完成。
items.description: SpaceX 在历史性 IPO 数天后，同意以 600 亿美元股票收购 AI 编程初创公司 Cursor，旨在帮助其围绕 xAI 构建的 AI 部门追赶主要 AI 实验室。此前 Cursor 正接近完成一轮 20 亿美元融资，估值 500 亿美元，投资方包括 Andreessen Horowitz、Thrive 和 Nvidia。SpaceX 在 IPO 期间向投资者表示，其 AI 产品可寻址市场达 26 万亿美元。交易预计于今年第三季度完成。

items.title: DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，估值超 500 亿美元
items.summary: 中国 AI 初创公司 DeepSeek 完成首轮外部融资，募资超 500 亿元人民币（约 74 亿美元），估值超 500 亿美元。投资结构特殊：多数投资者将资金投入 CEO 梁文锋管理的有限合伙企业，无投票权且锁定期五年；仅国资 AI 基金直接投资并保留投票权。创始人梁文锋个人投入约 200 亿元，腾讯和宁德时代为主要外部投资者。梁文锋表示优先基础 AI 研究与 AGI 开发，将继续开源。DeepSeek 去年初凭 V3、R1 模型获全球关注，今年 4 月发布运行于华为芯片的最大开源权重模型 V4，并将 V4 Pro 永久折扣 75%，输入价格约为 OpenAI GPT-5.5 的 1/11，输出价格约为 1/35。

trendEntities.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
normalizedKeys: title:google cloud 推出 okf v0 1 供应商中立的 markdown 规范 为 ai 智能体提供结构化上下文

items.title: Google Cloud 推出 OKF v0.1：供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文
items.summary: Google Cloud 发布 Open Knowledge Format （OKF） v0.1，一种供应商中立的 Markdown 规范，为 AI 智能体提供结构化上下文知识。OKF 将知识表示为带 YAML 前置元数据的 markdown 文件目录，每个概念对应一个文件，通过 `type`、`title`、`description` 等少量保留字段实现互操作。无需专有服务、SDK 或运行时，目录可托管在 GitHub、以 tarball 传输或挂载到任意文件系统。OKF 旨在解决组织内部知识碎片化问题--表结构、指标定义、runbook 等散落在不同 catalog 和 wiki 中，各厂商方案互不兼容。遵循最少意见原则，只强制 `type` 字段，生产者和消费者可独立实现。使用场景包括数据团队将 BigQuery 表定义导出为代码、为智能体存储 incident runbook、跨组织知识交换等。

trendEntities.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
normalizedKeys: title:cartesia 发布 sonic 3 5 与 ink 2 实时语音模型

items.title: Cartesia 发布 Sonic 3.5 与 Ink 2 实时语音模型
items.summary: Cartesia 推出 Sonic 3.5 和 Ink 2 两个模型，作为单一实时语音栈，分别负责文本转语音和语音转文本。Ink 2 在 Artificial Analysis 的流式语音转文字排行榜上排名第一。Sonic 3.5 在实时文本转语音中位列榜首，首音频延迟约 82ms。Cartesia 成为目前唯一同时拥有 #1 听与说模型的提供商。

items.title: 微软旗下GitHub遭遇AI算力短缺，转而向AWS寻求支持
items.summary: 微软旗下GitHub面临AI算力短缺，微软因此转向亚马逊AWS寻求计算资源支持。原文来自Hacker News热门讨论，标题为"Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch"。

items.title: AI 版支付宝官宣开启邀测：右滑打开"阿宝"，官方放出 100 个邀请码
items.summary: 支付宝今日开启 AI 版邀测，用户可右滑进入新版界面，在对话框或语音中输入指令，由"阿宝"助手代办事项。以查询公积金为例，阿宝自动匹配对应小程序和服务入口，用户点击确认即可完成，将多步跳转折叠为一句指令。所有涉及资金变动和支付的环节均需用户本人确认。首批放出 100 个邀请码。

trendEntities.title: 蚂蚁百灵发布 Ling & Ring 2.6 技术报告
normalizedKeys: title:蚂蚁百灵发布 ling ring 2 6 技术报告

items.title: 蚂蚁百灵发布 Ling & Ring 2.6 技术报告
items.summary: 蚂蚁百灵发布 Ling & Ring 2.6 技术报告，系统公开 Ling-2.6-flash、Ling-2.6-1T 和 Ring-2.6-1T 的架构、预训练、后训练及 Agent 强化学习细节。三款模型采用 Hybrid Linear Attention 架构，将 Lightning Attention 与 MLA 以 7：1 比例结合。Ling-2.6-flash 在 4×H20 硬件上解码速度达 340 tokens/s，Ling-2.6-1T 在 Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index 上 token efficiency 较前代提升约 4 倍。Ring-2.6-1T high 在 PinchBench 得 87.60，ClawEval 得 63.82。三款模型均已开源。
items.description: 蚂蚁百灵发布 Ling & Ring 2.6 技术报告，系统公开 Ling-2.6-flash、Ling-2.6-1T 和 Ring-2.6-1T 的架构、预训练、后训练及 Agent 强化学习细节。三款模型采用 Hybrid Linear Attention 架构，将 Lightning Attention 与 MLA 以 7：1 比例结合。Ling-2.6-flash 在 4×H20 硬件上解码速度达 340 tokens/s，Ling-2.6-1T 在 Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index 上 token efficiency 较前代提升约 4 倍。Ring-2.6-1T high 在 PinchBench 得 87.60，ClawEval 得 63.82。三款模型均已开源。

trendEntities.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
normalizedKeys: title:毕业生陷入ai检测荒诞循环 手写摘要被判99 ai率 ai写部分0

items.title: 毕业生陷入AI检测荒诞循环：手写摘要被判99%AI率，AI写部分0%
items.summary: 当前毕业生面临论文AIGC率检测荒诞困境。学生手写摘要被判定99%AI率，纯AI写部分却为0%。学校要求AIGC率不超40%，学生用Claude反复修改并花上百元检测费（维普20元/篇，知网/万方2元/千字符），最终降至36.1%。答辩时老师要求改回学术表达，AI率回升至37.21%。同一论文在不同平台检测结果差异巨大（48%、44%、59%）。部分平台提供降重收费服务，少数学校已改用AI使用声明表替代一刀切检测。

trendEntities.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
normalizedKeys: title:教育部 阳光志愿 信息服务系统全新升级上线 智能筛选志愿 数据权威可信

items.title: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统全新升级上线：智能筛选志愿，数据权威可信
items.summary: 教育部"阳光志愿"信息服务系统今日全新升级上线，依托招生、学籍、就业等海量官方数据，免费为考生和家长提供志愿填报服务。系统支持31个省区市本专科普通批次志愿筛选，输入高考成绩、位次及个性化条件即可快速生成合理参考方案。AI助手"智慧小招"24小时在线解答政策规则。平台数据由高校直接报送、官方核验，真实可靠。同时推出专业倾向测评和21项生涯测评工具，帮助学生认清特长、规划未来。

items.title: Variable-Width Transformers
items.description: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. items.summary: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. In this work, we empirically investigate nonuniform capacity allocation across network depth by proposing a $\times$-shaped > <former architecture. This design maintains wider early and late layers while narrowing the middle layers, utilizing a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism. Across decoder-only language models ranging from 200M to 2B parameters (dense) and 3B parameters (MoE), our > <former consistently outperforms parameter-matched uniform baselines on language modeling loss. By reducing the average layer width, this architecture also requires fewer overall FLOPs (22% reduction under fitted loss-matched scaling curves) and smaller KV cache memory and I/O cost (15% reduction). In analysis, we show that this bottleneck structure results in qualitatively different representations in residual streams. Overall, our results demonstrate that nonuniform width allocation can result in more resource-optimal scaling of language models.
items.author: Zhaofeng Wu, Oliver Sieberling, Shawn Tan, Rameswar Panda, Yury Polyanskiy, Yoon Kim

items.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
items.description: Reproducing research results from papers and released code is central to scientific progress. Existing works have introduced benchmarks to evaluate whether LLM agents can assist with reproducibility, but they are difficult to scale due to their reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation and evaluation. items.summary: Reproducing research results from papers and released code is central to scientific progress. Existing works have introduced benchmarks to evaluate whether LLM agents can assist with reproducibility, but they are difficult to scale due to their reliance on substantial manual effort for data curation and evaluation. We introduce ReproRepo, a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that leverages human-raised GitHub issues as naturally occurring supervision on realistic reproduction blockers. We instantiate ReproRepo on 1,149 recent machine learning papers from major conferences and evaluate four frontier model-agent configurations. Our results show that LLM agents, even without executing code, can identify many real-world reproducibility problems from paper-repository pairs: the best agent in our study, namely Codex with GPT-5.5, surfaces at least one semantically related human-reported blocker for ~90% of papers in the study. Further analysis shows that agents are particularly effective for surfacing visible failures and identifying the right semantic region, but may still be insufficient in exact localization. ReproRepo can serve as a reusable, scalable framework for future evaluations of LLM agents on real-world reproducibility auditing. Our code is released at https://github.com/LithiumDA/ReproRepo.
items.author: Shanda Li, Qiuhong Anna Wei, Jingwu Tang, Valerie Chen, Nihar B Shah, Tim Dettmers, Yiming Yang, Ameet Talwalkar
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, benchmark, evaluation

items.title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
items.description: Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) requires embodied agents to explore and locate target objects without any prior training. To this end, recent methods leverage foundation models. But they typically rely on static priors and lack adaptation, which leads to repeated errors and costly trial and error. items.summary: Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) requires embodied agents to explore and locate target objects without any prior training. To this end, recent methods leverage foundation models. But they typically rely on static priors and lack adaptation, which leads to repeated errors and costly trial and error. In this paper, we propose a self-evolving ZS-OGN framework that enables continuous test-time improvement. Specifically, we build an agentic rule memory by extracting actionable knowledge from past trajectories. Then, we propose a retrieval strategy based on upper confidence bound, selecting effective rules by balancing semantic relevance and historical success. In addition, we introduce a memory-guided preflection module that forecasts potential outcomes before action, reducing inefficient exploration. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms existing zero-shot baselines, achieving a 10.1\% improvement in success rate with fewer unnecessary steps.
items.author: Qi Chai, Wenhao Shen, Nanjie Yao, Yue Xia, Kaiyong Zhao, Jie Ma, Guosheng Lin, Hao Wang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, agentic

items.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
items.description: With sophisticated cyber-attacks becoming increasingly prevalent, modern networks require intelligent autonomous cyber-defense agents trained via Reinforcement Learning (RL). items.summary: With sophisticated cyber-attacks becoming increasingly prevalent, modern networks require intelligent autonomous cyber-defense agents trained via Reinforcement Learning (RL). These agents employ neurosymbolic approaches such as behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs) to learn, reason, adapt, and implement security rules while maintaining critical operations. However, these autonomous networks are partially observable systems, i.e., the cyber-attacker's (red agent's) actions are not observable, making it difficult for the defender to predict red actions, learn red policies, or assess the attacker's intrusion levels. To address this, we propose a Policy Learning Technique using imitation learning to learn policies for partially observable RL agents with discrete states and discrete actions. We apply this technique in an autonomous cyber environment to predict red agent's actions from network observations and defender actions. Integrated with a neurosymbolic cyber-defense agent, our method effectively handles different red policies and achieves high prediction accuracy across diverse simulated scenarios.
items.author: Ankita Samaddar, Sandeep Neema, Daniel Balasubramanian, Xenofon Koutsoukos
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent

items.title: Darshana Graph: A Parallel Commentary Corpus for Comparative Indian Philosophy, with Stylometric and Exploratory Graph Analyses
items.description: We introduce Darshana Graph, a corpus of over 125,000 text records spanning classical Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, drawn from public-domain and openly licensed translations of sources including the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Sutras, principal Upanishads, the Pali Canon, and core Jain texts. items.summary: We introduce Darshana Graph, a corpus of over 125,000 text records spanning classical Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain philosophical traditions, drawn from public-domain and openly licensed translations of sources including the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Sutras, principal Upanishads, the Pali Canon, and core Jain texts. Its distinctive contribution lies in a structurally unique subset of roughly 8,500 Hindu and Jain records in which the same root verse or sutra is aligned across eighteen historical commentators representing five schools of Vedanta and other darshanas, enabling direct comparison of how independent interpretive traditions read identical source material. To our knowledge, no publicly available resource provides comparable cross-commentator alignment at this scale. We present two analyses built on this corpus. First, a transparent stylometric comparison requiring no machine learning measures argumentative style through scriptural citation density, explicit refutation rate, and sentence complexity. It finds a moderate negative correlation between citation density and refutation rate, a marked increase in refutation rate across three commentators in a related doctrinal lineage, and measurable genre-level differences within the Pali Canon itself. Second, we describe a constrained large language model pipeline that extracts typed philosophical relationships between concepts using a predefined relation vocabulary and deterministic post-hoc validation. The resulting graph surfaces cross-school disagreement patterns while also revealing important extraction limitations, including cases where an independent embedding-based analysis disagrees with the graph-derived findings. We release the full corpus, extracted relationship graph, and all source code.
items.author: Joy Bose
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, embedding, alignment

Second, we describe a constrained large language model pipeline that extracts typed philosophical relationships between concepts using a predefined relation vocabulary and deterministic post-hoc validation. The resulting graph surfaces cross-school disagreement patterns while also revealing important extraction limitations, including cases where an independent embedding-based analysis disagrees with the graph-derived findings. We release the full corpus, extracted relationship graph, and all source code.

trendEntities.title: Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization: Teacher in Prompts, Not Gradients
normalizedKeys: title:zone of proximal policy optimization teacher in prompts not gradients

items.title: Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization: Teacher in Prompts, Not Gradients
items.description: Knowledge distillation transfers a teacher's competence to a small student but is brittle in the small-student regime: forcing the student to imitate logits from a much larger teacher concentrates it on the teacher's sharpest modes, hurting generalization on benchmark families beyond the training corpus. Reinforcement learning (RL) avoids logit imitation by training on the student's own rollouts. However, on questions where every rollout fails-yielding zero advantage and being silently discarded
items.summary: Knowledge distillation transfers a teacher's competence to a small student but is brittle in the small-student regime: forcing the student to imitate logits from a much larger teacher concentrates it on the teacher's sharpest modes, hurting generalization on benchmark families beyond the training corpus. Reinforcement learning (RL) avoids logit imitation by training on the student's own rollouts. However, on questions where every rollout fails-yielding zero advantage and being silently discarded-injecting a stronger teacher's response into the policy gradient breaks the on-policy assumption and induces drift. We introduce Zone of Proximal Policy Optimization (ZPPO), inspired by Vygotsky's zone of proximal development, which keeps the teacher inside the prompt rather than the policy gradient. On hard questions, ZPPO constructs two reformulated prompts: a Binary Candidate-included Question (BCQ) pairs one correct teacher response with one incorrect student response as anonymized candidates the student must discriminate, and a Negative Candidate-included Question (NCQ) aggregates the student's wrong rollouts into a single prompt to surface their shared failure modes. A prompt replay buffer recirculates each hard question until it either graduates-the student's mean rollout accuracy on it reaches half- or is FIFO-evicted under finite capacity, amplifying BCQ and NCQ inside the student's current zone of proximal development. On the Qwen3.5 family at four student scales (0.8B-9B) with a 27B teacher, post-trained as vision-language models and evaluated on a 31-benchmark suite (16 VLM, 10 LLM, 5 Video), ZPPO outperforms off/on-policy distillation and GRPO, with the largest gains at the smallest scale.
items.author: Byung-Kwan Lee, Ximing Lu, Shizhe Diao, Minki Kang, Saurav Muralidharan, Karan Sapra, Andrew Tao, Pavlo Molchanov, Yejin Choi, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Ryo Hachiuma

trendEntities.title: Rethinking Dataset Distillation for Classification: Do Distilled Sets Outperform Coresets?
normalizedKeys: title:rethinking dataset distillation for classification do distilled sets outperform coresets

items.title: Rethinking Dataset Distillation for Classification: Do Distilled Sets Outperform Coresets?
items.description: Dataset distillation (DD) has emerged as a prominent approach in data centric machine learning, aiming to synthesize compact training sets for efficient training by compressing the information in large datasets into a small number of synthetic samples. However, DD methods are often evaluated under inconsistent evaluation protocols, ranging from standard ERM to single/multi-teacher supervision, making it difficult to isolate the effectiveness of distilled data from evaluation. Moreover, many prio
items.summary: Dataset distillation (DD) has emerged as a prominent approach in data centric machine learning, aiming to synthesize compact training sets for efficient training by compressing the information in large datasets into a small number of synthetic samples. However, DD methods are often evaluated under inconsistent evaluation protocols, ranging from standard ERM to single/multi-teacher supervision, making it difficult to isolate the effectiveness of distilled data from evaluation. Moreover, many prior methods claim that DD outperforms data pruning approaches such as coreset selection (CS), based on the assumption that restricting condensed datasets to subsets of real samples fundamentally limits their expressiveness. In this work, we critically evaluate DD methods through large-scale experiments using standardized datasets and evaluation protocols to assess their intrinsic effectiveness. We benchmark seven state-of-the-art (SOTA) DD methods on ImageNet-1K, ImageNet100, and ImageNette, using three widely adopted training protocols against three CS strategies. Our results show that while some DD methods fail to outperform even simple random subsets, the SOTA DD approaches are comparable to or worse than coresets on large-scale datasets and incur a substantially higher cost for construction. Beyond accuracy, we also evaluate the representativeness, diversity, and quality of condensed sets, and find that coresets consistently achieve better coverage of the original data distribution. These findings highlight the limited practical advantages of current DD methods and show that coresets remain competitive and are often a more computationally efficient alternative for data-centric learning.
items.author: Trisha Mittal, Akshay Mehra, Joshua Kimball
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Fixed-Point Reasoners: Stable and Adaptive Deep Looped Transformers
normalizedKeys: title:fixed point reasoners stable and adaptive deep looped transformers

items.title: Fixed-Point Reasoners: Stable and Adaptive Deep Looped Transformers
items.description: Looped architectures provide an inductive bias toward learning step-by-step procedures for tasks that require compositional reasoning. The number of effective layers reached by looping determines the quality of the solution these models find. Like deep architectures, looped architectures are prone to a signal propagation problem induced by depth as the halting decision is postponed. In this paper, we address this signal propagation issue using pre-norm layers and residual scaling. Building on th
items.summary: Looped architectures provide an inductive bias toward learning step-by-step procedures for tasks that require compositional reasoning. The number of effective layers reached by looping determines the quality of the solution these models find. Like deep architectures, looped architectures are prone to a signal propagation problem induced by depth as the halting decision is postponed. In this paper, we address this signal propagation issue using pre-norm layers and residual scaling. Building on these architectural modifications, we propose FPRM, a Transformer-based Fixed-Point Reasoning Model that uses fixed-point convergence as an end-to-end halting mechanism in a looped architecture. We show that fixed-point halting allows FPRM to adapt its compute to task difficulty. FPRM is effective on common reasoning benchmarks, namely Sudoku, Maze, state-tracking, and ARC-AGI.
items.author: Sajad Movahedi, Vera Milovanović, Shlomo Libo Feigin, Alexander Theus, Thomas Hofmann, Valentina Boeva, T. Konstantin Rusch, Antonio Orvieto
